steps.title: Rewrite both equations in standard form
steps.content:
Equation 1: Start with $48x - 72y = 30y + 24$. Collect like terms on one side: $48x = 102y + 24$, which rearranges to $48x - 102y = 24$.

Equation 2: Start with $ry = \frac{1}{6} - 16x$. Add $16x$ to both sides: $16x + ry = \frac{1}{6}$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines condition to solve for r
steps.content:
A system of linear equations has no solution when the two lines are parallel but distinct. In standard form, two lines are parallel if the ratios of their $x$- and $y$-coefficients are equal. Set up that proportion:
$$\frac{48}{16} = \frac{-102}{r}$$
Simplify the left side: $3 = \frac{-102}{r}$. Cross-multiply: $3r = -102$. Divide by 3: $r = -34$.
To confirm these lines are distinct (not identical), check the constant ratio: $\frac{24}{\frac{1}{6}} = 24 \cdot 6 = 144$. Since $144 \neq 3$, the lines are indeed parallel but non-coincident, so there is no intersection.
